title: Trace Flush and Export Flow
interaction_flow: 1. Application → DDTrace\Tracer: flush() [triggered at request end or manually]
2. DDTrace\Tracer::flush() → checks: config['enabled'], if false → return early
3. DDTrace\Tracer::flush() → (web only) addUrlAsResourceNameToSpan(rootSpan) if DD_TRACE_URL_AS_RESOURCE_NAMES_ENABLED
4. DDTrace\Tracer::flush() → logDebug('Flushing {count} traces, {spanCount} spans') using dd_trace_closed_spans_count(): int
5. DDTrace\Tracer::flush() → transport->send($this) [delegates to configured Transport impl]
6. DDTrace\Tracer::flush() → scopeManager->close()
--- Transport::Internal path (default, background writer) ---
7. Internal::send(TracerInterface $tracer) → checks DD_TRACE_GENERATE_ROOT_SPAN env config
8. Internal::send() → (if !DD_TRACE_GENERATE_ROOT_SPAN) DDTrace\flush() [C extension call, closes all spans & sends]
9. DDTrace\flush() [C ext] → serializes spans, buffers via dd_trace_buffer_span(array $traceArray): bool
10. DDTrace\flush() [C ext] → triggers background writer: dd_trace_coms_trigger_writer_flush(): int
11. BackgroundWriterThread → serializes buffered spans to msgpack payload
12. BackgroundWriterThread → dd_trace_send_traces_via_thread(int $numTraces, array $curlHeaders, string $payload): bool
13. BackgroundWriterThread → HTTP POST to Datadog Agent (application/msgpack, /v0.4/traces)
--- DebugTransport path (test/debug only) ---
14. DebugTransport::send(Tracer $tracer) → $tracer->getTracesAsArray()
15. DDTrace\Tracer::getTracesAsArray() → dd_trace_serialize_closed_spans(): array [C ext, auto-closes open spans]
16. DebugTransport::send() → array_merge($this->traces, tracesArray) [stores locally for test assertions]
--- Synchronous flush alternative ---
17. Application → dd_trace_synchronous_flush(int $timeout = 100): void [blocking, waits up to $timeout ms]
--- Force-close all spans variant ---
18. Application → dd_trace_close_all_spans_and_flush(): void [drops/closes open spans depending on DD_AUTOFINISH_SPANS, then flushes]
key_relationships: DDTrace\Tracer implements DDTrace\Contracts\Tracer (interface with flush(), getTracesAsArray())
DDTrace\Tracer::getTracesAsArray() wraps dd_trace_serialize_closed_spans() C ext function

key_functions.DDTrace\\flush(): C ext. Closes all spans and force-sends finished traces to agent. Part of DDTrace namespace.
key_functions.dd_trace_buffer_span(array $traceArray):: Internal C ext. Serializes and buffers spans for background writer. Input is dd_trace_serialize_closed_spans() format.
key_functions.dd_trace_coms_trigger_writer_flush(): in: Internal C ext. Signals background writer to flush buffered spans to agent.
key_functions.dd_trace_send_traces_via_thread(int $num: Internal C ext. Sends msgpack-encoded payload to background sender buffer. Only supports exactly 1 trace per call.
key_functions.dd_trace_closed_spans_count(): int: C ext. Returns count of closed (completed) spans tracked by tracer. Used for debug logging.
key_functions.dd_trace_serialize_closed_spans(): array: C ext. Serializes closed spans as arrays; auto-closes any open spans (destroys open span stack).
key_functions.dd_trace_synchronous_flush(int $timeout: Blocking synchronous flush. Waits up to $timeout ms for all spans to be sent.
key_functions.dd_trace_close_all_spans_and_flush(): vo: Force-finishes all spans and flushes. Drops open spans if DD_AUTOFINISH_SPANS=false, else closes them.
key_functions.dd_trace_internal_fn(string $functionNam: Executes internal writer functions: init_and_start_writer, shutdown_writer, synchronous_flush, set_writer_send_on_flush, etc.

transport_implementations.Internal: Default transport. Delegates to C extension DDTrace\flush(). setHeader() is no-op (background sender ignores headers). Only flushes if !DD_TRACE_GENERATE_ROOT_SPAN.
transport_implementations.DebugTransport: Test transport in DDTrace\Tests namespace. Accumulates traces in-memory ($traces array) via getTracesAsArray(). Supports setHeader()/getHeaders() for assertion. No actual HTTP call.
transport_implementations.Noop: Silent no-op transport for disabled tracing.

serialization: Spans are encoded as msgpack (application/msgpack). dd_trace_serialize_msgpack(array): bool|string is used for manual msgpack serialization. SpanData::meta_struct values are double-encoded (msgpack inside msgpack).
auto_flush_config: datadog.trace.auto_flush_enabled INI setting controls whether flush is triggered automatically at request end. Set to 1 by integrations like Laravel, Symfony, Roadrunner, Frankenphp for long-running processes.
background_writer_internals: C-level background thread. Internal API: init_and_start_writer, ddtrace_coms_next_group_id, ddtrace_coms_buffer_span, ddtrace_coms_buffer_data, shutdown_writer, set_writer_send_on_flush, test_consumer, test_writers, test_msgpack_consumer, synchronous_flush, root_span_add_tag.
important_notes: flush() is no-op if tracer is disabled (config['enabled']=false)
dd_trace_serialize_closed_spans() destroys the open span stack — must be called only at end of trace lifecycle
dd_trace_send_traces_via_thread only supports exactly 1 trace per call (limitation noted in docblock)
DebugTransport is for testing only; it stores traces in memory for assertion via getTraces()
Synchronous flush is blocking and accepts a timeout in milliseconds (default 100ms)
dd_trace_close_all_spans_and_flush behavior depends on DD_AUTOFINISH_SPANS: false=drop open spans, true=close them
Internal transport setHeader() is a no-op; background sender does not accept custom HTTP headers
OpenTracer1\Tracer::flush() and OpenTracer\Tracer::flush() both delegate to the inner DDTrace\Tracer::flush()
